seo.title: Self-Hosting implementation checklist
seo.description: A practical checklist for shipping Self-Hosting projects safely.

content.intro: This checklist provides a technical framework for transitioning self-hosted infrastructure from a development state to a production-ready environment, focusing on security, durability, and operational stability.

items.title: Disable Password Authentication
items.description: Modify /etc/ssh/sshd_config to set PasswordAuthentication to no and ensure only SSH keys are used for access.

items.title: Configure Firewall Rules
items.description: Use UFW or iptables to block all incoming traffic except for ports 80, 443, and your custom SSH port.

items.title: Enable Unattended Upgrades
items.description: Install the unattended-upgrades package and configure it to automatically apply security patches to the base OS.

items.title: Install Fail2ban
items.description: Deploy Fail2ban with jails for SSH and any exposed web services to automatically ban IPs with multiple failed login attempts.

items.title: Non-Root Service Execution
items.description: Verify that all Docker containers and system services are running as non-privileged users rather than root.

items.title: Automate SSL/TLS Certificates
items.description: Configure Caddy, Traefik, or Nginx with Let's Encrypt to ensure automatic 90-day certificate rotation.

items.title: Enforce HTTPS Redirection
items.description: Verify that all port 80 traffic is redirected to port 443 with a 301 Permanent Redirect status code.

items.title: Implement HSTS Headers
items.description: Add the Strict-Transport-Security header to all reverse proxy responses to prevent protocol downgrade attacks.

items.title: Internal Docker Networking
items.description: Ensure databases and backend services are on private Docker networks and not mapped to host ports via the 'ports' directive.

items.title: DNS TTL Optimization
items.description: Lower DNS TTL to 300 seconds during initial migration to allow for rapid IP failover if the VPS fails.

items.title: Pin Image Tags
items.description: Replace all ':latest' tags in docker-compose files with specific version numbers or SHA hashes to prevent breaking changes on restart.

items.title: Define Resource Limits
items.description: Set 'cpus' and 'memory' limits in the compose file for every container to prevent a single service from crashing the host.

items.title: Configure Health Checks
items.description: Implement the 'healthcheck' instruction in Docker files to allow the orchestrator to restart unresponsive containers.

items.title: Automate Image Cleanup
items.description: Set up a cron job or systemd timer to run 'docker system prune' to prevent disk exhaustion from dangling images.

items.title: Configure Log Rotation
items.description: Set the Docker log-driver to 'json-file' with 'max-size' and 'max-file' limits to prevent logs from filling the disk.

items.title: Off-site Backup Automation
items.description: Use Rclone, Borg, or Restic to sync volume data to an S3-compatible bucket or secondary server every 24 hours.

items.title: Database Dump Verification
items.description: Verify that database backups are performed using native tools (e.g., pg_dump) rather than just copying live data files.

items.title: Restore Dry-Run
items.description: Perform a full restore of the application and database to a fresh VPS to verify backup integrity and documentation.

items.title: Volume Encryption
items.description: Ensure sensitive data volumes are stored on encrypted partitions (LUKS) if the VPS provider supports it.

items.title: Automated Snapshot Schedule
items.description: Enable provider-level snapshots (e.g., Hetzner/DigitalOcean) as a secondary recovery layer for the entire OS.

items.title: External Uptime Monitoring
items.description: Configure an external service like Uptime Kuma or BetterStack to ping the public endpoint and alert on 5xx errors.

items.title: Disk Space Threshold Alerts
items.description: Set up a script or agent to send a notification (Slack/Discord/Email) when disk usage exceeds 80%.

items.title: OOM Killer Monitoring
items.description: Configure log alerts for 'Out of Memory: Kill process' messages in dmesg to identify resource-starved containers.

items.title: Centralized Log Access
items.description: Deploy a lightweight log aggregator like Dozzle or a Vector/Loki stack to view logs without SSH access.

items.title: SSL Expiry Monitoring
items.description: Verify that your monitoring tool tracks the SSL certificate expiration date independently of the auto-renewal system.

items.title: Data Residency Verification
items.description: Confirm that the VPS region is physically located within the EU or required jurisdiction for GDPR compliance.

items.title: Administrative 2FA
items.description: Enable Two-Factor Authentication on all administrative panels (Coolify, Portainer, or application dashboards).

items.title: VPN-Only Admin Access
items.description: Move administrative tools behind a Wireguard or Tailscale tunnel so they are not reachable via the public internet.

items.title: Secrets Management
items.description: Remove all plaintext passwords from docker-compose files and use .env files with restricted 600 permissions.

items.title: Privacy Policy Update
items.description: Update the application's privacy policy to reflect the specific self-hosted infrastructure and data sub-processors used.
